pages.full_text:
table for supper. The food was delicious
1lse flottant.
    Simon + Eve would give this campsite
5star, lake + lots of walks, but John + Charles
insist on only 4 star - the view's blocked by
trees, you have to pay for showers + the croissants
pages.notes: Page contains a glued-in restaurant receipt from 'La Vieille Auberge, 58120 Chaumard' dated 31-07-2002 (Wednesday), showing a meal for 4: 4 menu tradition. 49.60, 1 badoit 4.70, 1 gamay Pinot 11.00, TOTAL 65.30 (F428.34). Also contains a small photo of a white building (the restaurant) and what appears to be a scenic/landscape postcard photo. The handwritten text begins mid-sentence, continuing from the previous page. 'Ilse flottant' likely refers to 'ile flottante' (floating island dessert). The text cuts off mid-sentence at the bottom ('the croissants') continuing on the right page.

pages.full_text:
were stale. They are sold, after ordering,
from a depot by a woman who also runs
the fishing tackle shop.
    __Thursday__
        More drizzle during the night + morn.
John walked up to get the bread. Charles
boated. Eve swam. After breakfast at 10.20.
we left.
    Charles + Simon shopped at a supermarket.
At 1.50. there was a metalic banging so
we stopped + had lunch. Simon discovered
that one of the straps holding on the water
tank was loose. A nut worked loose +
weakened a thread in the body of the van,
possibly. He tied it on with string.
    We approached Clung, + Eve was
pleased to go. John said "Oh yes, Clung:
I've seen him in 2 films." and that about
summed up John's + Charles' interest in the
Abbey. It was poorly sign-posted once we
had paid for the tickets + the adolescent
boys were a pain to drag round. It was
difficult to figure out where to go + we
pages.notes: The place name 'Clung' almost certainly refers to Cluny (Abbaye de Cluny), a famous Benedictine abbey in Burgundy, France. The diarist spells it 'Clung' and John makes a film reference joke ('I've seen him in 2 films') treating 'Cluny' as a person's name. The Thursday date is estimated as 2002-08-01 based on the receipt on the facing page being dated 31-07-2002 (Wednesday), making Thursday the following day. The page number '65' is visible top-right. Text continues beyond the bottom of the page.
